protein_name: Essential for the control of the cell cycle at the G1/S (start) transition. Belongs to the cyclin family. Cyclin E subfamily. Highly expressed in testis and placenta. Low levels in bronchial epithelial cells. 3 alternatively spliced human isoforms have been reported. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.

raw_fields.Protein type: Activator; Cell cycle regulation; Nuclear receptor co-regulator
raw_fields.Cellular Component: cyclin E1-CDK2 complex; cyclin-dependent protein kinase holoenzyme complex; cytoplasm; cytosol; microtubule organizing center; nucleoplasm; nucleus
raw_fields.Molecular Function: cyclin-dependent protein serine/threonine kinase regulator activity; kinase activity; protein binding; protein kinase binding
raw_fields.Biological Process: cell division; G1/S transition of mitotic cell cycle; positive regulation of G1/S transition of mitotic cell cycle; positive regulation of mesenchymal stem cell proliferation; protein phosphorylation
raw_fields.Alt. Names/Synonyms: CCNE; CCNE1; cyclin E1; cyclin Es; cyclin Et; G1/S-specific cyclin-E1; pCCNE1
